channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Hundreds protest in Kyiv over Zelenskyy’s dismissal of defence minister
item.description: Mykhailo Fedorov has been credited with reforming defence procurement and tackling corruption.

item.title: Fire at orphanage in Algeria kills 11 people, including children
item.description: Eleven people died and 19 were injured after a fire swept through a state-run child welfare home in east Algiers.

item.title: Climate funding: Who pays for the transition?
item.description: Developed nations are cutting climate funding, but developing countries still need billions to adapt.

item.title: ‘Safe passage’: The fatal ambiguity at the heart of the Hormuz MoU
item.description: The vague language of the US-Iran MoU made the present escalation in the Strait of Hormuz all but inevitable.

item.title: What is cyclospora? Diarrhoea symptoms, foods linked to US outbreak
item.description: Cyclospora cases are rising across the US. Here's what to know about symptoms, spread, treatment and prevention.

item.title: More than 500 feared dead after reports of boats capsizing off Myanmar
item.description: The IOM and UNHCR say two boats carrying mostly Rohingya passengers departed Rakhine State in late June.

item.title: Photos: Messi’s Argentina stun England to reach World Cup final vs Spain
item.description: Messi inspires Argentina’s dramatic late comeback win over England to reach final against Spain.

item.title: Decades of denial: Death certificate for disappeared reopens Kashmir wounds
item.description: After years of searching and court battles, a judge declares what Junaid Rashid already believed: His father was dead.

item.title: Yemen is edging closer to renewed confrontation
item.description: The Iranian aircraft incident has exposed a fragile calm that may not survive a wider regional escalation.

item.title: FIFA World Cup final: Date, time, halftime show, prediction and Trump
item.description: From Donald Trump's attendance to the match winner, the short guide before football’s global showpiece in New Jersey.

item.title: Why Italy’s Genoa bridge collapse led to one of its biggest criminal trials
item.description: Eight years after 43 people were killed, judges rule in the first trial over the Genoa bridge collapse.

item.title: Vance says Epstein had connections to US, Israeli intelligence
item.description: The US vice president says Epstein 'seemed' to be connected to elements of the 'Israeli deep state'.

item.title: US releases video of strikes on Iran
item.description: US releases video of strikes on Iran

item.title: US strikes Iran, tanker, as Tehran hits Kuwait, Jordan: What’s the latest
item.description: Strikes have hit closer to Tehran for the first time since the latest wave of attacks started.

item.title: Greece pins hopes on heat-seeking satellites to predict wildfires
item.description: The country, which suffers each summer, builds an arsenal of high-tech surveillance to help pre-position firefighters.

item.title: US launches new attacks on Iran as Tehran targets Gulf sites
item.description: Iran's Health Ministry has said at least 35 people have been killed and more than 300 injured since fighting resumed.

item.title: Watch FIFA World Cup: Argentina rallies past England to reach final
item.description: Latest match highlights, reaction and previews from the FIFA World Cup 2026 in North America.

item.title: ‘Escaping Beijing’: Why some young Chinese are quitting the capital
item.description: A slowing economy and rising prices are making it harder for young Chinese to thrive in the city.

item.title: Pete Hegseth says US military to start testosterone screening for over 30s
item.description: US Defense Secretary says plan is 'about restoring and optimising ... natural capabilities' of troops.

item.title: Trump’s intelligence chief nominee won’t say Biden won 2020 election
item.description: US President Trump’s nominee for top intel official, Jay Clayton, evaded directly stating that Biden won 2020 election

item.title: UK proposes voluntary overnight social media curfew for older teens
item.description: Latest government measure aimed at curbing digital harm comes after under-16 social media ban announced last month.

item.title: Argentina’s Falklands banner sparks controversy at World Cup
item.description: Argentina players held up a banner declaring ‘Las Malvinas son Argentinas’ after beating England.

item.title: Former US Ambassador says Iran miscalculated Trump’s resolve
item.description: Nightly strikes between the US and Iran have been the heaviest since the Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) agreement.

item.title: Senior Chinese delegation visits North Korea for talks
item.description: China's fourth-highest-ranked official, Wang Huning, held talks with a top official of North Korea's governing party.

item.title: South Korea’s international adoptees seek justice, not homecoming
item.description: South Korea's government has acknowledged wrongs, but advocates say accountability is still lacking.
